items.title: Third H5N1 bird flu case confirmed as virus found in South Australia

children.content: A third confirmed case of the deadly H5N1 bird flu has been found in a migratory bird on Australia's southern coast.

gallery.altText: A black-feathered bird with a while bill that has a greenish tip sitting on a towel.
A person wearing PPE facing away from the camera on a mountain

thumbnail.altText: A black-feathered bird with a while bill that has a greenish tip sitting on a towel.

items.title: Bird flu prompts Papua New Guinea to ban Australian chicken and egg imports

children.content: Australia's largest chicken meat buyer, Papua New Guinea, suspends Australian poultry and egg imports due to the H5N1 bird flu outbreak, even though Australian authorities say there is no evidence the virus has spread.

image.altText: Dozens of hens with glossy brown feathers and red plump combs under a clear blue sky.

items.title: Headline inflation eases more than expected but RBA rate hike risk remains

children.content: Headline inflation has eased to an annual rate of 4 per cent in May, down from 4.2 per cent in April, according to the latest ABS data.
The better-than-predicted figure comes after sharp falls in petrol and diesel prices.
The Australian share market finished the day higher despite a major sell-off across US tech, AI and semiconductor stocks which has resulted in steep losses on Wall Street. Follow live.

image.altText: A shopping trolley full of essentials items like fruit, water, and eggs

items.title: Minister acknowledges Australian housing market is experiencing 'correction'

children.content: Clare O'Neil has acknowledged that the property market is undergoing a correction, but says the government's proposed tax changes are not the sole cause.

image.altText: A woman wearing headphones looks forward toward a radio microphone.

items.title: Pauline Hanson says Socceroos represent her vision of monoculture

children.content: Hanson says she is "delighted" monoculturalism is being debated and says the Australian soccer team represents her vision for a national "monoculture". Follow live.

image.altText: Pauline Hanson and other One Nation senators in the senate.

items.title: Police to review case of Epstein accuser Virginia Giuffre

children.content: The interactions Jeffery Epstein accuser Virginia Giuffre had with Australian police before her death will be reviewed, WA's police commissioner confirms.

gallery.altText: woman with white blonde hair and wearing a navy suit looks down the camera

thumbnail.altText: woman with white blonde hair and wearing a navy suit looks down the camera

items.title: Karl Stefanovic's interview with Tommy Robinson taken offline

children.content: Today show host-turned-podcaster Karl Stefanovic has had his interview with far-right extremist Tommy Robinson taken down from social media and podcast platforms.

image.altText: Two men in black on a street.

items.title: Angus Taylor says he believes in 'a version' of multiculturalism

children.content: Opposition Leader Angus Taylor will not break a decades-long bipartisan position of supporting multiculturalism, saying he still supports "a version" of it after dithering under questioning yesterday.

image.altText: Opposition leader Angus Taylor wearing a suit and speaking at a press conference.

items.title: Socceroos should make 'harsh' call for final group game, Schwarzer says

children.content: As the Socceroos prepare to face Paraguay in their final FIFA World Cup group game, Aussie great Mark Schwarzer says now is the time to bring captain Mat Ryan back into the team.

articles.title: When do the Socceroos play Paraguay and what time is the game?

children.content: The Socceroos have one more match in the group stage as they look to make it to the knockout stages of the World Cup. Here's everything you need to know.

image.altText: Irankunda slides along a green pitch on his knees.

articles.title: 'Doesn't seem right': Why Socceroos won't play for a draw against Paraguay

children.content: A draw would be enough for the Socceroos to progress from their World Cup group, but according to defender Jason Geria, playing for anything less than a win does not sit right with this group.

image.altText: Jordan Bos #5 of Australia is tackled by Hakan Calhanoglu #10 of Turkiye

articles.title: Quick hits: Trump's special job and commentator banned for outburst

children.content: Cristiano Ronaldo makes a bit of World Cup history as Gianni Infantino offers an update on the future of the hydration breaks and Donald Trump is given role at this year's final. Here are today's quick hits.

image.altText: Donald Trump, Cristiano Ronaldo, Gianni Infantino

gallery.altText: Socceroo Mark Schwarzer shouts encouragement to his team-mates during the 2010 FIFA World Cup qualifying match

thumbnail.altText: Socceroo Mark Schwarzer shouts encouragement to his team-mates during the 2010 FIFA World Cup qualifying match

items.title: UN maritime body starts enacting plan to move sailors out of Gulf

children.content: An evacuation plan to enable hundreds of ships to sail through the Strait of Hormuz is being enacted, according to the UN's International Maritime Organization.

image.altText: Lots of small boats sit on the water of the  Strait of Hormuz. Huge brown mountains loom in the background.

items.title: Pilot dies in plane crash near Northam, north-east of Perth

children.content: Police say a light plane crashed in Malabaine on Wednesday morning, with the pilot and sole occupant of the aircraft found dead at the scene.

image.altText: Aerials of a light plane crash.

items.title: DRC Ebola outbreak grows to become one of worst recorded

children.content: There are now more than 1,000 confirmed cases of Ebola in the Democratic Republic of Congo and 267 people have died, local officials say.

gallery.altText: a person in ppe walks through a dirt road refugee camp

thumbnail.altText: a person in ppe walks through a dirt road refugee camp

items.title: 'Limited resources': Christian Brothers paying $1.7m a week to abuse victims

children.content: The Congregation of Christian Brothers Oceania Province has revealed it was seeking to sell off its 36 properties to pay abuse victims, who would be treated as creditors.

image.altText: A wooden cross at the top of a building with blue sky behind it.

items.title: Matt Wright sued for negligence after airboat allegedly crashed into tree

children.content: A man who says he suffered a "psychiatric injury" after witnessing injuries sustained by his wife in a 2023 airboat crash is suing tour operator and Netflix star Matt Wright for negligence.

image.altText: A man in a button-up shirt and jeans, walking towards the courthouse while holding his wife's hand.

items.title: Farming couple who met at bus stop celebrate 70 years of love on the land

children.content: Farming couple Bill and Val Collins share their secrets to a long marriage as they celebrate their platinum wedding anniversary.

gallery.altText: An elderly couple look lovingly at each other

thumbnail.altText: An elderly couple look lovingly at each other

items.title: Heartbroken Daryl Braithwaite will miss everything about performing

children.content: The ARIA Hall of Fame inductee speaks emotionally to the ABC about his decision to give up live performances, saying it has broken his heart to see how appreciative people have been.

image.altText: Daryl Braithwaite

items.title: Looksmaxxing is everywhere, but have you heard of mummymaxxing?

children.content: Also known as mommymaxxing and mommaxxing — depending on which internet rabbit hole you're going down — mummymaxxing focuses on optimising motherhood, and experts warn it could be harmful.

image.altText: mum and dad sit with two children reading a book in very stylised setting

items.title: What stolen party funds bought Nicola Sturgeon's husband

children.content: Court documents have revealed what former Scottish first minister Nicola Sturgeon's husband Peter Murrell bought with funds he embezzled from the party over 12 years.

gallery.altText: A man who is middled aged, bald and in glasses stares straight ahead

thumbnail.altText: A man who is middled aged, bald and in glasses stares straight ahead

items.title: Outgoing British PM holds meeting with likely successor

children.content: Sir Keir Starmer and the man who has essentially forced him to resign as prime minister, Andy Burnham, have held their first face-to-face meeting.

image.altText: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer looks sideways.

items.title: 'Historic blunder': US Senate votes to seek to prevent further military action in Iran

children.content: It is the 10th time the Senate has tried to stop the war, and the outcome, on a vote of 50–48, is a stunning turnaround from past efforts.

image.altText: A group of politicians surround a podium with hands raised.

items.title: What this coal mine sale says about the future of the industry

children.content: An Indonesian mining company has agreed to purchase a bankrupt Queensland company's assets, which include the Vulcan coal mine in the Bowen Basin.

image.altText: Image of a coal mine from above with what appears to be activity

items.title: Coalition's renewables push 'chaos' for NSW energy plan, Minns says

children.content: Under the proposal, a Sydney City Renewable Energy Zone would be established to support greater use of solar and battery storage across the city, while the New England project would be scaled back and its proposed 500-kilovolt transmission line corridor scrapped.

image.altText: A generic image of a technician installing solar panels on a roof.

items.title: Murray-Darling Basin communities call for balance in review

children.content: Communities across the Murray-Darling Basin are calling for a better balance, as irrigators say water buybacks are hurting their communities.

gallery.altText: A view of the River Murray from a cliff, flanked by trees. The sky is blue.

thumbnail.altText: A view of the River Murray from a cliff, flanked by trees. The sky is blue.

items.title: Bondi terror attack hero pleads not guilty to alleged assault on father

children.content: Ahmed Al Ahmed has pleaded not guilty to allegations he assaulted and intimidated his father during an incident in March.

image.altText: Man in dark suit, white shirt looking down, another man with beard standing next to him

items.title: Family left 'shattered', 'traumatised' by woman's murder, court told

children.content: Jessie James Tumaliuan has been sentenced to more than two decades in jail for the violent murder of his estranged wife in March last year.

image.altText: Czarina Gatbonton Tumaliuan smiles at the camera standing in a city at night.

items.title: Family shares significant update on Sydney shark attack victim's condition

children.content: A Sydney mother mauled by a shark at Coogee Beach almost a fortnight ago has spoken her first words after briefly waking from an induced coma.

image.altText: Sydney mother and Coogee local, Leah Stewart, who was mauled by a shark holds a baby

items.title: The 'uncontrolled' police department behind fatal shooting of Perth girl

children.content: The grandfather of Australian schoolgirl Hania Ahmed recounts the aftermath of her fatal shooting, as a senior Pakistani lawyer takes aim at the "uncontrolled" police department at the centre of her death.

image.altText: Headshot of a father and his young daughter holding up a graduation certificate.

items.title: Drug charges laid after women allegedly try to evade police after exiting Spirit ferry

children.content: Two women are alleged to have had traffickable quantities of meth and cocaine in their car when they evaded police upon exiting the Spirit of Tasmania ferry in Devonport.

gallery.altText: Tasmania Police Detective Inspector Michelle Elmer holding a large quantity of illicit drugs.

thumbnail.altText: Tasmania Police Detective Inspector Michelle Elmer holding a large quantity of illicit drugs.

avatar.alt: A woman with straight, fair, shoulder-length hair smiles. She wears a black top.

items.title: Qantas A380 to be inspected urgently after cracks found

children.content: Airbus says it will inspect 16 A380 planes, including one Qantas plane, after cracks were found in a key wing component.

image.altText: A Virgin and a Qantas airliner on a tarmac.

items.title: UN inquiry accuses Israel of deliberately targeting and killing Palestinian children

children.content: The Israeli government is condemning the inquiry and rejecting its findings, labelling the United Nations report "deeply flawed" and full of "errors and distortions" designed to push a "politically driven anti-Israel narrative".

image.altText: A young child looking through a makeshift tent's window.

items.title: IVF add-ons are common in Australia, but there's no proof most work

children.content: Experts urge clinics and specialists to "carefully consider" offering IVF add-on treatments as they can be seen as "implicit endorsement of benefit" when there's little evidence most work.

gallery.altText: A composite image of a woman smiling with a needle and a woman taking a selfie.

thumbnail.altText: A composite image of a woman smiling with a needle and a woman taking a selfie.

items.title: Australia launches world-first suicide prevention approach

children.content: Policymakers, mental health experts and those working on the front line will use the National Suicide Prevention Outcomes Map to gauge what services are working to prevent people from reaching critical crisis points.

image.altText: Jo Riley smiles while in a garden. She is wearing a colourful top and a necklace.

items.title: Port Moresby's water supply network showing 'signs of imminent failure'

children.content: A decades-old pipeline supplying water to nearly 1 million people in Papua New Guinea's capital is at risk of collapsing without urgent repairs, threatening to cut off more than half of the city's water supply.

image.altText: Water bursts from two points in a long concrete flume.

items.title: He's been out for 50 years, but many Gardening Australia fans wouldn't know

children.content: Gardening Australia star Jerry Coleby-Williams hid his identity on TV for 26 years, but has never been ashamed of being a gay man.
